name: Indiana County Community Action Program, Inco
description_short: Indiana County Community Action Program is a 56+ year old non-profit providing free food, housing, and financial assistance to low-income individuals and families in Indiana County, PA.
description_long:
Indiana County Community Action Program (ICCAP) was established in 1965 as a result of President Lyndon B. Johnson's War on Poverty legislation and the Economic Opportunity Act. The organization has spent over 56 years serving as a private, non-profit human service agency committed to mobilizing resources and empowering individuals toward self-sufficiency in Indiana County, Pennsylvania.

ICAP offers a comprehensive range of free assistance programs including food pantries, emergency food assistance, Pennsylvania Senior Food Box Program (nutrition-specific), emergency mortgage assistance, emergency shelter for families and individuals, and rental assistance for those experiencing homelessness or near-homelessness. The organization also operates Power Pack, a donation-based program distributing child-friendly non-perishable food to all 12 public schools throughout Indiana County. Their services are designed to address the most pressing challenges facing individuals and families in their service area, as informed by their Community Needs Assessment.

What distinguishes ICCAP is its 56-year track record of sustained community commitment and its extensive partnership network. The organization collaborates with other non-profit agencies, government agencies, businesses, faith-based organizations, consortiums, educational institutions, health service agencies, and financial institutions to expand their service capacity and reach. This multi-sector approach allows them to provide integrated solutions rather than siloed assistance.

ICAP is a legitimate free-help resource for Indiana County residents facing economic hardship. The organization is transparent about its mission, published contact information, and specific programs. However, services are geographically limited to Indiana County, PA only. Prospective clients should verify program eligibility and current availability by contacting them directly at (724) 465-2657 or help@iccap.net.
pros: 56+ years of established community presence and track record fighting poverty
Multiple free food assistance options including senior nutrition programs and school-based Power Pack distribution
Emergency housing assistance including mortgage help, emergency shelter, and rental assistance for homeless/near-homeless individuals
Extensive partnerships with non-profits, government, businesses, and faith organizations expanding service capacity
Free community needs assessment conducted to ensure programs address real local challenges
Transparent organizational structure with published board members and directors for accountability
Operates in all 12 public schools throughout Indiana County for maximum community reach
cons: Services limited exclusively to Indiana County, PA residents—no assistance available outside this geographic area
Website lacks detailed program eligibility requirements, application processes, or waitlist information
No clear documentation of funding sources, annual budget, or financial transparency reports publicly available
Limited online intake or application capability—requires phone contact or in-person office visit
No specific information about program wait times, response rates, or service level commitments
best_for: Indiana County, PA residents experiencing food insecurity needing food pantry or emergency assistance
Families and individuals facing housing instability, eviction, or homelessness in Indiana County
Low-income seniors in Indiana County seeking nutrition-specific food assistance programs
Families with school-age children in Indiana County public schools seeking food support through Power Pack
diagnosis: ICCAP is best for Indiana County, PA residents experiencing food insecurity, housing instability, or financial hardship who need free emergency assistance. The main caveat is that services are limited to Indiana County only, and detailed program eligibility and application procedures must be confirmed by contacting the organization directly.
services: Pennsylvania Senior Food Box Program (nutrition-specific food assistance)
Power Pack school-based food distribution (all 12 Indiana County public schools)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
